about.title: Seth Hirachand Gumanji Jain Trust, Mumbai
about.subtitle: Preserving Heritage, Empowering Future Generations
about.missionText: To serve the Jain community by providing quality education, secure accommodation, and spiritual nourishment to students and travelers, while preserving our rich cultural heritage and fostering values of compassion, non-violence, and selfless service for the betterment of society.
about.empowermentDesc: Providing quality education and accommodation to Jain students, enabling them to pursue academic excellence while staying rooted in their cultural values.
about.spiritualIntegrityDesc: Upholding the principles of Jain philosophy by creating an environment that nurtures spiritual growth, ethical conduct, and disciplined living.
about.serviceDesc: Extending selfless service to the Jain community and society at large through charitable initiatives, community welfare programs, and humanitarian support.
about.aboutPara1: The Seth Hirachand Gumanji Jain Trust, Mumbai, is a distinguished charitable institution dedicated to the welfare and upliftment of the Jain community. Founded over a century ago by the illustrious Jhaveri family of Bhindar, Rajasthan, the Trust has been a beacon of hope and service for generations.
about.aboutPara2: The Trust manages three key institutions: a Boys Hostel, a Girls Ashram, and a Dharamshala, all located in the heart of Mumbai. These facilities provide affordable and quality accommodation to Jain students pursuing higher education and to pilgrims visiting the city.
about.aboutPara3: With a deep commitment to preserving Jain values of non-violence, truth, and compassion, the Trust has continuously adapted to modern needs while maintaining its cultural roots. The institution operates under the guidance of a dedicated Board of Trustees who ensure transparency, accountability, and adherence to the founding principles.
about.timeline1852Desc: The seeds of the Trust were planted by the visionary Jhaveri family of Bhindar, Rajasthan, who recognized the need for community welfare in Mumbai.
about.timeline1900Desc: The Trust was formally established under the guidance of Seth Hirachand Gumanji, bringing together resources for the betterment of the Jain community.
about.timeline1914Desc: The flagship Boys Hostel was inaugurated on Lamington Road, Mumbai, providing affordable accommodation for Jain students pursuing higher education.
about.timeline1940Desc: Recognizing the importance of women's education, the Girls Ashram was established, offering a safe and nurturing environment for female students.
about.timeline1972Desc: The Dharamshala was opened to serve Jain pilgrims and travelers, providing comfortable lodging for those visiting Mumbai for religious and personal purposes.
about.timelinePresentDesc: Embracing modern technology to streamline hostel management, admissions, and communication while preserving the Trust's core values and heritage.
about.boysHostelDesc: Well-maintained hostel facilities for male students with modern amenities, study rooms, and a supportive community atmosphere.
about.girlsAshramDesc: A secure and nurturing residential facility for female students, equipped with all necessary amenities for comfortable living and focused study.
about.dharamshalaDesc: Comfortable guest accommodations for Jain pilgrims and travelers, located conveniently in Mumbai with easy access to temples and transport.

contact.title: Contact Us

faqPage.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faqPage.subtitle: Find answers to common questions about our hostels and services
faqPage.q1: Who can apply for hostel admission?
faqPage.a1: Hostel admission is open to students belonging to the Digambar Jain community who are pursuing their education in Mumbai. Applicants must provide valid community references and proof of enrollment at a recognised educational institution. Both undergraduate and postgraduate students are eligible to apply.
faqPage.a2: The admission process begins with submitting an application through our online portal. After submission, your documents will be verified by the hostel superintendent. Shortlisted applicants are then called for an interview with the trust committee. Upon approval, you will receive an admission offer with instructions for fee payment and room allocation.
faqPage.a3: The trust charges minimal fees to support students from the community. Fee amounts vary depending on the room type (shared or single occupancy) and the specific hostel. Scholarships and fee concessions are available for students demonstrating financial need. Detailed fee structures are shared upon receiving the admission offer.
faqPage.q4: How can I book a room at Dharamshala?
faqPage.a4: Rooms at Hirabaug Dharamshala can be booked through our online portal or by visiting the trust office in person. Advance booking is recommended, especially during festival seasons and peak travel periods. Stays are available for a maximum of 7 days and are open to members of the Jain community with valid identification.
faqPage.q5: Are meals provided in the hostel?
faqPage.a5: Yes, the hostel provides pure Jain vegetarian meals prepared under strict dietary guidelines. The mess operates on a fixed schedule for breakfast, lunch, and dinner. Special dietary requirements can be communicated to the warden. No outside non-vegetarian food is permitted on the premises.
faqPage.q6: How can I donate to the trust?
faqPage.a6: Donations to the trust can be made via bank transfer, cheque, or in person at the trust office. All donations are eligible for tax benefits under Section 80G of the Income Tax Act. The trust issues official receipts for all contributions. For large donations or corpus fund contributions, please contact the trust office directly.
faqPage.q7: What facilities are available for girls?
faqPage.a7: The R.R. Shravika Ashram is a dedicated facility for girls with women-only staff and management. The ashram features 24/7 security with CCTV surveillance, a separate study hall, recreation room, and prayer hall. Strict visitor policies ensure a safe and comfortable environment. A female warden resides on the premises at all times.

trustees.title: Board of Trustees
trustees.heritageSubtitle: A legacy of service, faith, and dedication spanning over a century and a half.
trustees.keyManagementSubtitle: The dedicated leaders who guide the Trust with wisdom and commitment.
trustees.livingLegacyText: The Seth Hirachand Gumanji Jain Trust stands as a testament to the enduring power of faith, service, and community. From its origins in the small town of Bhindar to its present-day impact in Mumbai, the Trust continues to honor its founders' vision by empowering students, supporting pilgrims, and preserving the rich cultural heritage of the Jain community. As it embraces digital transformation, the Trust remains steadfast in its commitment to the values that have guided it for over 170 years.

dpdpPolicy.title: Data Protection & Privacy Policy
dpdpPolicy.subtitle: Data Protection and Privacy Principles (DPDP) Act Compliance
dpdpPolicy.privacyBanner: Your privacy rights are protected under Indian law. This policy outlines how we collect, use, and protect your data.
dpdpPolicy.weCollectDesc: Personal, academic, and financial data for hostel management
dpdpPolicy.weUseDesc: Only for admission, stay management, safety, and compliance
dpdpPolicy.weKeepDesc: During stay + 1-7 years based on data type
dpdpPolicy.policyUpdatesText: This policy was last updated on January 1, 2025. We may update this policy periodically to reflect changes in practices or regulations.
dpdpPolicy.consentRenewal: For students, consent renewal is required every 6 months as part of the renewal process. You will be notified when updates require new consent.
dpdpPolicy.policyQuestions: For questions about this policy, contact the Data Protection Officer

apply.title: Choose Your Accommodation Type
apply.subtitle: Select the hostel type you wish to apply for. Each option has specific requirements and facilities.
apply.dataProtectionDesc: Your personal information is protected under the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act. By proceeding with your application, you consent to our data usage policies for admission processing.
apply.boysHostelDesc: Modern accommodation for male students with focus on academic excellence, character building, and spiritual growth in a safe, disciplined environment.
apply.girlsAshramDesc: Safe and nurturing environment for female students with enhanced security, dedicated study areas, and focus on holistic development.
apply.dharamshalaDesc: Spiritual retreat and temporary accommodation for pilgrims and visitors seeking peaceful stay with simple amenities and community facilities.
apply.noAccountRequiredDesc: You don't need to create an account to apply. An account will be created automatically only after final approval of your application.
apply.trackApplicationDesc: After submission, you'll receive a tracking number via SMS/Email to monitor your application status throughout the admission process.
apply.documentPreparationDesc: Keep your documents ready: Birth Certificate, Educational Records, Community Recommendation, and Recent Photographs.

track.title: Track Your Application
track.subtitle: Enter your tracking ID and mobile number to view your application status
track.trackingIdHelper: Enter the tracking number from your application confirmation
track.mobileHelper: Enter the mobile number used during application
track.secureTrackingDesc: Your tracking ID and mobile number are used only to verify your identity and retrieve your application status. All data transmission is encrypted and complies with DPDP Act, 2023.
track.needHelp: Need help? Contact our admissions team at
track.errorBothRequired: Please enter both tracking ID and mobile number
track.errorNotFound: Application not found with this tracking ID
track.errorMobileMismatch: Mobile number does not match the application
track.errorVerifyFailed: Failed to verify application. Please try again.
track.errorResendFailed: Failed to resend OTP. Please try again.
